propertyDescription: Single-storey brick veneer, 3br/2ba, 185 m² total. Kitchen and adjacent hallway affected, 22 m² combined.
causeOfLoss: Burst braided stainless flexi-hose under kitchen sink. Estimated 6–8 hours undetected. Source confirmed shut off at inline isolator by plumber prior to attendance.
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Kitchen cold-water flexi-hose (DN15 braided stainless, estimated age 9 years) failed at compression fitting, releasing clean water under mains pressure for approximately 6–8 hours before discovery. **IICRC S500:2025 §4.2** — source identified as Category 1 (clean water). Inline isolator confirmed shut off and plumber sign-off sighted. No sewage or grey-water contamination detected. Water pathway: kitchen cupboard base → vinyl plank floor → hallway timber-look laminate → subfloor void via expansion gaps.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Wet vacuum extraction: kitchen floor 12 m², hallway 10 m² — approximately 180 L removed **§8.2**
- Kitchen base cabinets: 2 × lower cabinets water-damaged, removed for drying assessment
- Contents protection: refrigerator and dishwasher shifted to dry zone; kitchen contents boxed and relocated
- Subfloor inspection via access hatch — timber bearers and joists show moisture migration; max moisture reading 38% WME on timber (baseline target ≤16% WME) **§8.3**
- Documentation: 24 photographs taken; moisture map sketch completed

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Kitchen (12 m²)**
- Vinyl plank flooring (click-lock, semi-porous backing): fully saturated — removal required
- Kitchen base cabinet carcasses (2 units, particleboard): particleboard swollen — total loss, removal required
- Plasterboard wall lining behind sink cupboard: lower 150 mm wet, moisture reading 85% WME — flood cut at 300 mm required
- Concrete slab sub-base: surface moisture present, 22% WME — dry in place

**Hallway (10 m²)**
- Timber-look laminate flooring (HDF core): saturated — delamination evident, removal required
- Timber subfloor bearers visible via hatch: 38% WME — drying in place with directed airflow
- Plasterboard skirting (lower 100 mm): elevated moisture 42% WME — spot replacement post-dry

Material classifications per IICRC S500:2025 **§6**: vinyl plank = semi-porous (Category B); HDF laminate = semi-porous; particleboard = porous; plasterboard = porous; concrete slab = non-porous.

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline readings at time of inspection (Day 1, 0900 hrs):
- Temperature: 27.4°C
- Relative Humidity (RH): 68%
- Grains per Pound (GPP): 142
- Dew point: 21.1°C
- Reference outdoor conditions: Temp 26°C, RH 72%

Drying target per IICRC S500:2025 **§10.2**: achieve psychrometric balance and drive indoor RH below 50% before demobilisation. Specific moisture targets per §11.4: plasterboard ≤17% WME; timber subfloor ≤16% WME; concrete slab ≤4% WME.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Affected floor area: 22 m². IICRC S500:2025 equipment ratios applied.

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 1 × 70L/day unit | 1 per 40 m² (S500:2025) |
| Air movers (axial) | 4 × 1.5 kW units | 1 per 15 m² = 1.5 → round up + 1 per wet wall panel |
| HEPA air scrubber | Not required | Category 1 — no contamination |

Air mover placement: 2 × kitchen floor at 45° to wet wall panel; 1 × hallway floor directed at subfloor hatch; 1 × directed at opened wall cavity (300 mm flood cut).

Electrical load check per AS/NZS 3012:2019: total connected load = 4 × 1.5 kW + 1 × 0.8 kW (dehumidifier) = 6.8 kW. 10 A general power outlet rated 2.4 kW — **dedicated 15 A circuit used; total draw 6.8 kW = 28% of 240V/20A circuit capacity**. Compliant (≤80% of 24 kW available).

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring schedule: 09:00–10:00 daily. Readings logged in RestoreAssist drying goal module.

- Moisture meter readings: plasterboard flood-cut edges (3 points), subfloor timber (4 points), hallway subfloor (2 points)
- Psychrometrics: temperature, RH, GPP logged at central monitoring point each visit
- Equipment checks: dehumidifier tank/drain, air mover positioning verified, electrical connections checked
- Photo documentation: minimum 4 photos per visit showing equipment in place and readings
- Target thresholds per IICRC S500:2025 **§11.4**: plasterboard ≤17% WME; timber ≤16% WME; achieved when 3 consecutive readings ≤ target

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

Drying complete when all of the following conditions are met per IICRC S500:2025 **§11.4**:
- All plasterboard remnants ≤17% WME (3 consecutive daily readings)
- All timber subfloor ≤16% WME
- Concrete slab surface ≤4% WME
- Indoor RH ≤50%

Estimated drying duration: **5–7 days** (Class 2, Category 1, QLD summer ambient conditions).

Upon validation: Drying Goal Certificate issued in RestoreAssist. Reinstatement trigger: hydraulic plumber replacement of flexi-hose, then vinyl plank and laminate replacement by licensed builder. Plasterboard replacement at flood-cut line. Cabinet replacements to be assessed for insurance claim submission.

propertyDescription: Single-storey brick veneer, 4br/2ba, 220 m². Laundry, hallway, master bedroom, and two minor bedrooms affected. Total affected area: 72 m².
causeOfLoss: Washing machine supply hose failed at machine inlet fitting. Estimated 4–6 hours undetected. Water source is warm-water machine connection — classified Category 2 (grey water) per IICRC S500:2025 §5.3.
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Front-load washing machine cold-water inlet hose failed at machine connection point. Water at 40°C (heated cycle in progress) classified as Category 2 (grey water — thermal degradation risk plus surfactant contamination). Source isolated at laundry isolation valve. Loss pathway: laundry concrete floor → hallway carpet → master bedroom carpet → bedroom 2 carpet → bedroom 3 vinyl plank. Wall cavity intrusion confirmed in hallway — moisture detected behind plasterboard at 60 cm height. **IICRC S500:2025 §4.2 / §5.3**.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Wet vacuum extraction — all carpet areas: approximately 340 L recovered **§8.2**
- Carpet and underlay assessment: Category 2 — full carpet and underlay removal required across all wet rooms per S500:2025 §5.3 (porous materials saturated with Category 2 water)
- Carpet removal: master bedroom 18 m², bedroom 2 12 m², hallway 14 m² — waste bagged and removed
- Underlay removal: same areas — underlay confirmed saturated
- Content protection: furniture elevated on foam blocks in bedroom 3 (vinyl plank — dry in place assessment)
- EPA-registered antimicrobial applied to all exposed subfloor surfaces post-carpet removal **§5.3**
- 47 photographs taken; moisture map completed

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Laundry (6 m²)**
- Concrete slab: surface saturation, 28% WME — dry in place
- Plasterboard lower 200 mm wet — flood cut at 450 mm required

**Hallway (14 m²)**
- Carpet + underlay: removed (Cat 2 — disposal)
- Timber subfloor: 52% WME at joists — elevated drying required
- Plasterboard wall cavity moisture detected up to 580 mm — flood cut at 600 mm required

**Master bedroom (18 m²)**
- Carpet + underlay: removed (Cat 2 — disposal)
- Particleboard subfloor: 61% WME — drying in place
- Plasterboard lower 150 mm wet — flood cut at 300 mm

**Bedroom 2 (12 m²)**
- Carpet + underlay: removed (Cat 2 — disposal)
- Particleboard subfloor: 44% WME

**Bedroom 3 (22 m²)**
- Vinyl plank flooring: 12% WME surface — dry in place

Material references: IICRC S500:2025 **§6** material categories applied.

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline (Day 1, 1100 hrs):
- Temperature: 28.6°C
- RH: 74%
- GPP: 160
- Dew point: 23.4°C

High ambient RH expected to impede drying — dehumidification critical. Drying targets: timber subfloor ≤16% WME, particleboard ≤15% WME, plasterboard ≤17% WME per **§11.4**.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Affected floor area: 72 m² (excluding bedroom 3 dry-in-place vinyl).

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 2 × 100 L/day units | 1 per 40 m² (72 m² ÷ 40 = 1.8 → 2 units) |
| Air movers (axial) | 8 × 1.5 kW units | 1 per 15 m² = 4.8 + 4 flood-cut wall panels = 8 total |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | 1 × 600 m³/h unit | Mandatory Category 2 per S500:2025 |

Electrical load: 8 × 1.5 kW + 2 × 1.0 kW + 1 × 0.7 kW = 14.7 kW. Distributed across 2 × 20A circuits. Load = 61% per circuit. Compliant AS/NZS 3012:2019 (≤80%).

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring: 09:00 daily. Full psychrometric log, moisture grid readings per room. Equipment checks: drain lines, filter inspection on HEPA scrubber every 48 hours. Antimicrobial re-application at Day 3 if RH remains above 60%.

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

Drying target achieved when: all subfloor readings ≤16% WME; all plasterboard remnants ≤17% WME; RH ≤50%; 3 consecutive compliant daily readings. Estimated duration: **8–10 days** (Class 3, Category 2, QLD ambient). Drying Goal Certificate issued in RestoreAssist on validation. Reinstatement: carpet supply and refit, plasterboard replacement, painting. Antimicrobial warranty documentation provided to insurer.

propertyDescription: Post-war Queenslander, single-storey elevated timber construction, 3br/1ba, 130 m². Bathroom, hallway, and kitchen affected. 38 m² total.
causeOfLoss: Blocked drain causing sewage backflow through floor waste in bathroom. Estimated 2-hour event. Water classified Category 3 (black water — raw sewage contamination) per IICRC S500:2025 §5.4.
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Sewer drain blockage (root intrusion, confirmed by plumber CCTV) caused sewage backflow through bathroom floor waste outlet. Category 3 water — grossly contaminated. Source rectified by licensed plumber (hydro-jet clearing + root treatment) prior to remediation commencement. **All porous structural materials in contact with Category 3 water must be removed — no exceptions (S500:2025 §5.4)**. Occupant vacate order issued. Health advisory provided in writing.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Category 3 PPE deployed: Level B — full-face respirator (combination cartridge P3/OV), impermeable Tyvek coveralls, double nitrile gloves, boot covers
- Sewage extraction: bathroom floor waste area — approximately 60 L removed by wet vacuum with HEPA-filtered exhaust
- Timber floorboards in bathroom: saturated (Cat 3 contact) — removal required, waste to be disposed as infectious waste
- Bathroom underfloor void: sewage pooling between bearers confirmed — full decontamination scope
- 38 photographs taken under full PPE; entry log maintained

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Bathroom (8 m²)**
- Timber floorboards (hardwood 19 mm): Cat 3 contact — remove and dispose as infectious waste
- Timber bearers/joists below: saturated with sewage — remove affected section and dispose
- Plasterboard lower wall lining: 2 walls affected, lower 400 mm — Cat 3 contact, remove and dispose
- Ceramic wall tiles (lower section): non-porous — decontaminate with EPA-registered antimicrobial in situ

**Hallway (12 m²)**
- Timber floorboards: sewage migration confirmed (18% WME moisture, odour present) — remove and dispose
- Plasterboard lower 200 mm: elevated moisture 34% WME — remove (Cat 3 precautionary)

**Kitchen (18 m²)**
- Timber floorboards: elevated moisture 24% WME — no sewage odour, border zone; HEPA vacuum, antimicrobial, and monitoring applied. Removal if moisture not resolved in 48 hours.

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline: Temp 24.8°C, RH 61%, GPP 126, Dew point 17.2°C.
Structure is elevated Queenslander — sub-floor ventilation present. Good natural drying potential post-decontamination. Drying targets: timber EMC ≤16% WME per §11.4.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Affected area post-removal: 20 m² (bathroom + hallway). Kitchen monitored separately.

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 1 × 70 L/day | 1 per 40 m² |
| Air movers | 3 × 1.5 kW | 1 per 15 m² + 1 × sub-floor direction |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | 1 × 600 m³/h | Mandatory Cat 3. Run continuously during active decontamination phase |

EPA-registered broad-spectrum antimicrobial: apply to all exposed structural surfaces, bearers, subfloor void walls, and non-porous tile surfaces after removal. Two applications required (Day 1 and Day 3).

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring twice daily during active decontamination phase (Days 1–3), then daily. Kitchen floorboards to be re-assessed at 48 hours — removal decision made based on readings. Odour assessment at each visit: persistent sewage odour = indicator of incomplete decontamination.

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

Clearance: independent hygienist ATP swab test of all treated surfaces required before sign-off (Cat 3 — no exceptions). All timber EMC ≤16% WME. Odour-free. Hygienist clearance certificate required before plumber, builder, and tile trades return to site. Reinstatement: replacement hardwood floorboards, plasterboard replacement, tiling match, plumbing reinstatement.

propertyDescription: Modern townhouse, 2-storey rendered masonry, 2br/1ba, 110 m². Single bathroom affected, 7 m².
causeOfLoss: Toilet cistern overflow — overflow fitting failed, Category 1 clean water. Tenant discovered within 30 minutes. Minimal spread.
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Toilet cistern ballcock valve failed, causing overflow via internal overflow pipe into pan and floor. Category 1 clean water. Loss contained to bathroom. Source isolated and plumber has replaced ballcock valve prior to attendance. Minimal spread — confined to bathroom floor and lower wall areas. **IICRC S500:2025 §4.2 / §5.2**.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Wet vacuum: bathroom floor 7 m², approximately 20 L extracted **§8.2**
- Contents protection: bathroom vanity contents removed
- Plasterboard lower 100 mm wet at bath screen wall — moisture 38% WME
- No structural penetration detected

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Bathroom (7 m²)**
- Ceramic tile floor (100×100 mm mosaic): non-porous, wet surface — dry in place, no removal required
- Plasterboard lower 100 mm (one wall): elevated moisture 38% WME — monitor; flood cut at 150 mm if not resolved by Day 3
- Grout lines: minor moisture intrusion — monitor, apply silicone grout sealer post-dry if required

Class 1 determination: limited moisture absorption, low-porosity materials predominant, no subfloor moisture detected. **S500:2025 §7.2**.

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline: Temp 25.2°C, RH 63%, GPP 130, Dew point 17.8°C. Small room, rapid drying expected with equipment placement.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Affected area: 7 m².

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 1 × 40 L/day | Minimum 1 unit |
| Air movers | 1 × 1.5 kW | 1 unit for 7 m² |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | Not required | Category 1, no contamination |

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring every 48 hours. Plasterboard lower wall moisture target ≤17% WME. If elevated on Day 3 → flood cut at 150 mm and re-assess. Psychrometric log daily.

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

All plasterboard ≤17% WME. Tile surface dry. RH ≤50%. Estimated duration: **3 days** (Class 1, Category 1). Drying Goal Certificate issued. No reinstatement required beyond minor grout inspection and silicone bead replacement at bath screen.

propertyDescription: Heritage Queenslander, single-storey elevated, 4br/2ba, 210 m². Kitchen, dining, and living room affected. Hardwood tongue-and-groove flooring throughout. 68 m² affected.
causeOfLoss: Hot water service burst in roof space. Water permeated ceiling insulation, wet plasterboard, and saturated hardwood T&G flooring below. 12-hour undetected event. Category 2 due to insulation contamination (fibreglass dust, roof-space debris).
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Electric hot water unit (250 L, estimated age 12 years) failed at pressure relief valve in roof space. Roof-space water classified Category 2 due to contact with fibreglass insulation, dust, and biological contaminants in roof void. Source isolated at cold-water isolation valve. Insurer notified. IICRC S500:2025 §4.2 / §5.3. Water pathway: roof-space concrete tile → sarking → insulation batts → plasterboard ceiling → cavity → hardwood flooring.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Emergency ceiling access: 3 × inspection holes cut to assess insulation saturation
- Saturated glasswool insulation (R3.5, 85 m² roof space): fully saturated — removal required, cannot be dried in situ
- Ceiling plasterboard: buckled and water-stained, 68 m² affected — removal required
- Hardwood T&G flooring: surface saturation and initial cupping detected across all 3 rooms
- Extraction: wet vacuum of standing water on flooring — approximately 120 L
- Content protection: furniture relocated to garage, rugs removed

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Ceiling void / roof space (68 m²)**
- Glasswool insulation R3.5: fully saturated — remove and dispose
- Plasterboard ceiling 10 mm: buckled, water-stained — remove and dispose

**Hardwood flooring (68 m²)**
- Hardwood tongue-and-groove (spotted gum, 80×19 mm, nail-down): cupping confirmed. Classified IICRC S500:2025 **Class 4** — low permeance, extended drying required **§7.5**
- Target EMC for spotted gum in QLD climate: 12–14% WME
- Acceptable moisture range at baseline: up to 35% WME (current readings: 42–56% WME)

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline: Temp 29.1°C, RH 78%, GPP 176, Dew point 25.0°C. Elevated ambient RH (QLD summer) is unfavourable for hardwood drying. Desiccant dehumidification considered for Phase 2 if LGR units insufficient by Day 7.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Affected area: 68 m².

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 2 × 100 L/day | 1 per 40 m² |
| Air movers (centrifugal, low-profile) | 6 × 1.5 kW | Floor-hugging position for hardwood; 1 per 15 m² = 4.5 → 6 for Class 4 |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | 1 × 800 m³/h | Mandatory Category 2. Fibreglass particles released during insulation removal |
| Desiccant dehumidifier (standby) | Standby option if RH target not achieved by Day 7 | Class 4 extended protocol §7.5 |

Hardwood drying strategy: low-profile centrifugal air movers placed flat on floor at 45° to boards. Cross-ventilation through open crawl space/sub-floor access ports. Hardwood boards re-nailed at affected joints to resist cupping pressure.

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring daily at 09:00. Hardwood moisture grid: 12 reading points across 3 rooms, daily. Cupping measurement logged weekly. Psychrometric log. Desiccant escalation decision: if Day 7 average hardwood reading > 25% WME, escalate to desiccant protocol. Expected drying period: 14–21 days per Class 4 protocol.

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

Drying goal: hardwood ≤14% WME (QLD EMC target per §11.4, adjusted for tropical climate). 3 consecutive compliant daily readings across all 12 grid points. Cupping assessment — minor residual cupping may require light sanding post-dry. Independent flooring specialist engaged if cupping > 2 mm variance across board width. Reinstatement: new plasterboard ceiling, R3.5 insulation replacement, painting. Hot water unit replacement by licensed plumber. Flooring: sand, re-coat if structurally sound, or board replacement if irreversible cupping.

propertyDescription: Modern double-storey rendered brick, 4br/2ba, 280 m². Upstairs ensuite flexi-hose failure causing first floor (kitchen, living, dining) saturation from above. 95 m² affected total.
causeOfLoss: Upstairs ensuite basin cold-water flexi-hose failed. Category 1 clean water migrated down through first-floor ceiling cavity, saturating plasterboard ceiling and wall linings on ground floor. Approx 10-hour event.
scope:
## 1. Water Source & Loss Mechanism

**Source confirmed:** Upstairs ensuite basin DN15 flexi-hose failed at tapware fitting. Category 1 clean water. Source shut at isolation valve behind vanity by owner. Water migration pathway: ensuite floor waste area → grout → concrete intermediate slab → cracked slab penetration at plumbing rough-in → wall cavity → ceiling space → ground floor ceiling → ground floor walls. Note: Category 1 classification maintained as water migrated via clean slab paths — no sewage or greywater contact confirmed.

## 2. Initial Emergency Services

- Bucket emergency: owner placed 12 buckets under active ceiling drips on arrival — approximately 60 L in buckets
- Extraction: ground floor carpet 35 m² extracted — approximately 280 L
- Carpet and underlay assessment: Category 1 but fully saturated — remove to access subfloor and allow structural drying (Class 3 — wall cavities affected)
- Upstairs ensuite: tile floor non-porous — dry in place post-source isolation
- Ground floor ceiling inspection: 6 × flood probe holes drilled at sagging points — water confirmed in ceiling cavity
- 52 photographs; moisture map completed

## 3. Affected Materials & Extent

**Ground floor (68 m² — kitchen, living, dining)**
- Carpet + underlay: removed (Class 3 wall cavity access required)
- Plasterboard ceiling 10 mm: 55 m² wet, moisture 72–95% WME — remove and dispose
- Plasterboard wall lining lower 600 mm: 4 walls affected — flood cut at 600 mm required
- Concrete slab ground floor: 18% WME surface — dry in place
- Timber stud framing: 42% WME — dry in place with directed airflow

**Upstairs ensuite (7 m²)**
- Ceramic tile floor: non-porous, wet — dry in place
- Intermediate concrete slab: 22% WME — monitor; desiccant if elevated at Day 7

## 4. Psychrometric Conditions

Baseline: Temp 26.4°C, RH 70%, GPP 149, Dew point 20.7°C. Class 3 structure — ceiling and upper wall saturated. Extended drying cycle expected due to concrete intermediate slab moisture.

## 5. Drying Equipment Setup

Ground floor affected area: 68 m².

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 2 × 100 L/day | 1 per 40 m² |
| Air movers (axial) | 10 × 1.5 kW | 1 per 15 m² = 4.5 + 5 flood-cut wall cavity panels + 1 ceiling cavity |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | Not required initially | Category 1; add if mould detected on Day 3 inspection |

Wall cavity drying: air movers angled through flood-cut openings. Ceiling drying: ceiling removed — direct airflow into exposed cavity.

## 6. Daily Monitoring Protocol

Monitoring daily. 20+ reading points: concrete slab (6), timber framing (8), plasterboard remnants (6). Ceiling cavity photographic check every 48 hours for secondary mould. Upgrade to Category 2 protocol if mould detected.

## 7. Drying Validation & Sign-Off

All timber framing ≤16% WME; concrete slab ≤4% WME; plasterboard remnants ≤17% WME. Estimated duration: **10–14 days** (Class 3, Category 1, two-storey concrete slab). Drying Goal Certificate issued in RestoreAssist. Reinstatement: ceiling replacement 55 m², wall lining replacement, carpet, painting. Upstairs flexi-hose replacement by licensed plumber.

propertyDescription: Tenanted brick veneer unit, ground floor of 2-storey complex, 2br/1ba, 85 m². Bathroom and laundry wall cavities affected. Visible mould growth 3.8 m² confirmed.
causeOfLoss: Chronic moisture from inadequately sealed shower recess allowing water intrusion into wall cavity. Combined with poor bathroom ventilation (exhaust fan disconnected at roof), mould Condition 3 established in wall cavities behind tiles.
scope:
## 1. Visual Assessment & Contamination Mapping

Room-by-room contamination mapping per IICRC S520 **§4.2**:

- **Bathroom** (5 m²): Condition 3. Visible mould growth on plasterboard behind removed tile (2.8 m²). Musty odour confirmed. Moisture reading: plasterboard 88% WME; timber stud framing 42% WME. Photo references: photos 1–18.
- **Laundry** (4 m²): Condition 2. Mould growth on lower 400 mm of plasterboard wall (1.0 m²). No tile backing. Moisture 62% WME. Photos 19–26.
- **Hallway adjacent to bathroom** (2 m²): Condition 1. No visible growth. Moisture readings normal. No remediation required.

Total affected area: **3.8 m²** visible growth.

## 2. Moisture Source Identification

Moisture source per IICRC S520 **§5.1 — must be confirmed and rectified before remediation commences**:

1. **Shower recess**: grout and silicone perished in 3 corners — water ingress confirmed via flood testing. **Rectification required**: licensed tiler to re-grout and re-silicone shower before remediation proceeds.
2. **Exhaust fan**: bathroom exhaust fan duct disconnected at roof — humid air recirculating into ceiling void. **Rectification required**: reconnect duct and clean fan impeller before remediation. Electrician to confirm fan operational.
3. **Laundry wall**: rising damp from slab edge detected — concrete moisture 24% WME at 100 mm above slab. Recommend plumber assess slab drainage.

Scope cannot proceed until Items 1 and 2 above are rectified and confirmed in writing by trades.

## 3. Contamination Boundary

Per IICRC S520 **§6.3** — minimum 500 mm clearance beyond visible growth boundary:

- Bathroom: visible growth 2.8 m² + 500 mm perimeter = remediation zone 5.2 m² plasterboard
- Laundry: visible growth 1.0 m² + 500 mm perimeter = remediation zone 2.1 m² plasterboard
- All materials within boundary listed for removal: plasterboard (7.3 m² total), timber noggings if wet >40% WME (2 noggings identified), any insulation within boundary (if present — to be confirmed on opening)

## 4. Containment Setup

Bathroom: Condition 3 — full critical barrier required per IICRC S520 **§7**:
- 200 µm poly sheeting floor-to-ceiling perimeter seal with duct tape
- 300 mm overlapping slit entry
- HEPA air scrubber establishing negative pressure differential ≥12.5 Pa — verify with manometer before work commences
- PPE: full-face respirator with P3 cartridge, Tyvek Level B coveralls, double nitrile gloves, boot covers

Laundry: Condition 2 — precautionary containment:
- 200 µm poly sheeting around work area
- N95/P2 respirator, nitrile gloves, disposable coveralls, eye protection

## 5. Remediation Scope

**Bathroom (Condition 3):**
- Remove ceramic wall tiles (2.8 m² tile area — careful removal to minimise airborne spore release)
- Remove plasterboard 5.2 m² (boundary zone)
- HEPA vacuum all exposed framing and adjacent surfaces
- Wet wipe all timber framing within boundary with EPA-registered antimicrobial (Benefect Botanical or equivalent)
- Rinse with clean water, dry with air mover, re-apply antimicrobial
- Inspect framing: 2 × noggings at 42% WME moisture — borate treatment applied (if structurally sound) or remove and replace

**Laundry (Condition 2):**
- Remove plasterboard 2.1 m²
- Wet wipe all exposed surfaces with EPA-registered antimicrobial
- HEPA vacuum post-treatment

All work per IICRC S520 **§8**.

## 6. Disposal Protocol

Per IICRC S520 **§8.4**:
- All removed materials double-bagged in 200 µm poly bags, sealed with duct tape
- Bags labelled: "Biological Waste — Mould Contaminated Material"
- Removed via designated exit pathway (not through habited areas)
- Disposal via council-approved general waste stream (non-infectious) — local council bio-waste guidelines confirm this classification for mould-contaminated building materials in QLD
- Waste removal to occur same day as remediation — no storage on site

## 7. Clearance Testing Recommendation

Post-remediation per IICRC S520 **§9**:
- Bathroom (Condition 3): independent third-party hygienist air sampling mandatory. Criteria: Condition 1 restored (spore counts within normal outdoor reference range, no elevated Stachybotrys or Chaetomium species)
- Laundry (Condition 2): visual inspection clearance acceptable; hygienist sampling recommended but not mandatory
- Reinstatement to commence only after written clearance certificate received
- Recommended hygienist: independent (not affiliated with restoration contractor) to maintain insurer credibility

propertyDescription: Commercial office building, 2-storey rendered masonry, 650 m² total. HVAC condensate failure caused ceiling cavity mould across Level 1 open-plan area, 210 m².
causeOfLoss: Air-handling unit condensate drain blocked — condensate overflow saturated ceiling insulation for estimated 6–8 weeks before discovery during maintenance inspection. Condition 3 mould throughout Level 1 ceiling void.
scope:
## 1. Visual Assessment & Contamination Mapping

Contamination mapping per IICRC S520 **§4.2**:

- **Level 1 ceiling void (210 m²)**: Condition 3. Widespread visible mould growth on underside of concrete deck, top surface of plasterboard tiles, and rockwool insulation batts. Air sample (independent hygienist) confirmed elevated Cladosporium and Aspergillus/Penicillium counts. Photos 1–64.
- **Level 1 open-plan office (210 m²)**: Condition 2 (settled spores via return air grilles). No visible growth on surfaces. Air sample elevated vs reference.
- **Level 2**: Condition 1. Normal spore counts. No remediation required.

Business disruption: Level 1 vacated. Occupant health advisory issued to building owner.

## 2. Moisture Source Identification

Per IICRC S520 **§5.1**:

1. HVAC condensate drain: blocked drain confirmed by HVAC contractor. Drain cleared, pan drained, AHU serviced.
2. AHU internal: mould growth confirmed on AHU internal components — full AHU decontamination and coil cleaning required before system restart (separate HVAC contractor scope).

Both items confirmed rectified by building services contractor in writing before remediation scope proceeds.

## 3. Contamination Boundary

210 m² ceiling void entire Level 1 is remediation zone. Boundary per §6.3 extends to all surfaces within cavity including underside of Level 2 slab, all service penetrations, all ductwork within cavity.

## 4. Containment Setup

Full critical barrier — commercial scale per IICRC S520 **§7**:
- Full Level 1 occupied area sealed with 200 µm poly barriers at stairwells and lift lobbies
- Industrial HEPA air scrubbers (3 × 1200 m³/h): negative pressure differential ≥12.5 Pa confirmed via 3-point manometer monitoring
- Decontamination unit (decon trailer or constructed anteroom): clean change area → dirty zone
- PPE: full-face respirator P3, Tyvek Level B, double nitrile, boot covers — full team
- HVAC system isolated and sealed at all grilles before work commences

## 5. Remediation Scope

- Remove all ceiling tile system (suspended grid + acoustic tiles, 210 m²)
- Remove all rockwool insulation within ceiling void (210 m²)
- HEPA vacuum all exposed surfaces: concrete deck underside, ductwork outer surfaces, conduit, pipe lagging
- Wet wipe all surfaces with EPA-registered antimicrobial × 2 applications
- AHU internal: decontaminate drain pan, coil surfaces, internal surfaces with antimicrobial (HVAC contractor)
- All ductwork within cavity: HEPA vacuum and antimicrobial wipe externally; internal duct survey recommended (separate scope)
- Reinstatement of ceiling void by licensed building contractor

Per IICRC S520 **§8**.

## 6. Disposal Protocol

Per IICRC S520 **§8.4**: all removed materials double-bagged in 200 µm poly. Estimated waste volume: 4 × 240 L skip bins. Commercial waste contractor engaged. Chain-of-custody documentation required for building owner's EHS records.

## 7. Clearance Testing Recommendation

Per IICRC S520 **§9**: independent hygienist air sampling mandatory (Condition 3, commercial occupancy). Sampling plan: 6-point air sample grid + 2 outdoor reference samples. Clearance criteria: indoor spore counts ≤ outdoor reference, no indicator species. Written clearance certificate required before HVAC restart and Level 1 reoccupation. Recommend 48-hour stabilisation period after works before sampling.

propertyDescription: Single-storey brick veneer, 3br/1ba, 165 m². Kitchen origin fire with smoke migration to living, dining, and hallway. Char zone: kitchen 12 m². Smoke zone: living/dining 32 m². Odour zone: bedrooms 64 m².
causeOfLoss: Unattended cooking — frying pan ignited, fire spread to overhead cabinetry before self-extinguishment. No suppression water applied. Dry smoke residue (fast-burning, high-temperature). Fire brigade attended, no structural suppression.
scope:
## 1. Fire Origin & Cause

**Origin confirmed:** Kitchen cooktop — unattended frying pan. Fire spread to overhead cabinetry (laminated particleboard) before self-extinguishing. Queensland Fire and Emergency Services (QFES) report reference: [to be inserted]. No structural fire involvement beyond kitchen. Dry smoke type (fast-burning, high-temperature fire) producing light, powdery soot. **IICRC S770 §4.1**.

## 2. Smoke Migration Mapping by Zone

Zone classification per IICRC S770 **§4.2**:

| Zone | Rooms | Classification | Visible Soot | Odour |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Char | Kitchen 12 m² | Char zone | Heavy carbon deposit | Intense |
| Smoke | Living/Dining 32 m² | Smoke zone | Light soot on walls, ceilings | Strong |
| Odour | Bedrooms (3) 64 m² | Odour zone | No visible soot | Noticeable |

Migration pathway: kitchen overhead cabinetry → open-plan ceiling void → living/dining ceiling rose and cornice → hallway → bedroom door gaps. HVAC not operating at time of fire — no duct contamination confirmed (all return grilles checked, no soot visible).

## 3. Affected Materials by Zone

**Char zone — Kitchen (12 m²)**
- Overhead cabinetry (4 units, laminated particleboard): complete loss — remove
- Plasterboard ceiling 10 mm (12 m²): smoke-stained, remove
- Plasterboard wall lining — 2 walls to 2.4 m height: light char, remove and replace
- Ceramic tile splashback: non-porous, clean in situ
- Concrete slab floor: non-porous, clean in situ

**Smoke zone — Living/Dining (32 m²)**
- Plasterboard ceiling 13 mm: light dry smoke soot — clean in situ
- Plasterboard walls: soot on upper sections — clean in situ
- Carpet (polyester): smoke odour absorbed — cleaning assessment (restore or replace)
- Fabric lounge suite: moderate smoke odour — hydroxyl chamber treatment

**Odour zone — Bedrooms (64 m²)**
- Plasterboard walls and ceilings: thermal fogging
- Carpet: odour treatment
- Wardrobes (hollow-core MDF doors): odour, thermal fogging treatment

## 4. Cleaning Methods by Material & Smoke Type

Dry smoke (fast-burning, powdery soot) per IICRC S770 **§8**:

1. **Dry sponge technique first** on all sooted surfaces — do not wet clean until dry soot completely removed (wet cleaning before dry sponge smears soot irreversibly)
2. HEPA vacuum all surfaces after dry sponge — capture residual soot particles
3. Wet clean with alkaline degreaser (pH 10–11) on hard surfaces after HEPA vacuum
4. Neutraliser rinse (pH 7) after alkaline clean
5. Char zone: surfaces too heavily sooted for cleaning — removal and replacement
6. Protein smoke present (cooking residue, minor): enzyme-based cleaner applied to cooktop surround and tile splashback in addition to above steps

## 5. Contents Inventory — Restore vs Replace Assessment

Per IICRC S770 **§9**:

- Kitchen overhead cabinets (4): total loss — removal included in structural scope
- Lounge suite (3-seater + 2-seater): restore — hydroxyl chamber treatment, vacuum, fabric deodoriser
- Bedding (3 bedrooms): restore — commercial laundering with enzyme treatment
- Electronics (TV, laptop): no soot contact — wipe down with antistatic cloth; functional check by owner
- Artwork (1 canvas print, living room): light soot — specialist dry-sponge by art conservator; restore
- Kitchen small appliances (microwave, toaster, kettle): char zone proximity — assess for soot ingress; recommend replacement for items with visible soot infiltration

## 6. Deodorisation Protocol

Per IICRC S770 **§10**:

- **Thermal fogging**: whole house including bedrooms, wardrobe internals, roof space access — 2-hour treatment. Re-entry interval: 4 hours post-fogging, ventilate for 2 hours before occupant re-entry
- **Hydroxyl generator**: living/dining zone — 3-day continuous treatment at 1 unit per 60 m². Lounge suite treated in-room
- **Ozone treatment**: NOT recommended with fabric contents present — risk of material degradation. Hydroxyl preferred for this job profile
- HVAC system: no contamination confirmed — recommend filter replacement as precaution before system operation resumes

## 7. Structural Drying

No fire suppression water applied. No structural drying required. Monitor plasterboard replacement areas for condensation during painting phase.

## 8. Clearance & Sign-Off

Per IICRC S770 **§11**:
- Visual inspection: all char and smoke zones to show no residual soot on any surface
- Odour assessment: panel of 3 technicians confirms no smoke odour at nasal height in any room
- Air quality test: NOT mandatory for this event (no char zone residue on porous materials post-removal; no suppression water). Recommend at owner/insurer request
- Reinstatement trigger: clearance sign-off → kitchen cabinetry replacement, ceiling/wall lining replacement, painting, new carpet to char and smoke zones

propertyDescription: Single-storey rendered brick, 3br/2ba, 175 m². South-east Queensland hailstorm event. Roof breach at ridge cap, 4 × displaced terracotta tiles. Water intrusion into master bedroom ceiling void, 22 m².
causeOfLoss: Severe hailstorm (BOM-tracked supercell, 29 January 2025). Ridge cap mortar cracked by hail impact, 3 × ridge cap sections dislodged. 4 × terracotta tiles displaced. Water ingress over approximately 72 hours before discovery by returning owner.
scope:
## 1. Breach Identification

**Confirmed breach points** per AS 4055:2021 assessment:

- Ridge line: 3 × mortar-set ridge cap sections dislodged (approx 1.8 m total ridge length exposed)
- Roof tiles: 4 × terracotta tiles displaced — 2 × cracked, 2 × intact but unseated
- Roof sarking: 1.8 m² tear at ridge line from tile displacement
- Valley flashing: intact, no damage
- Gutters: 2 × downpipe brackets damaged by hail — gutters sagging, minor blockage from debris
- No window or door frame damage detected

Structural assessment: roof battens and trusses visible through breach — no cracking or deflection observed. No engineering referral required at this stage.

## 2. Temporary Protection Measures (Make-Safe)

Make-safe completed within 24 hours of insurer notification per ICA obligations:

- 4 m × 6 m tarpaulin (200 µm commercial grade) placed over ridge breach
- Fixed with 40 × 19 mm timber battens, 65 mm galvanised bullet-head nails at 600 mm centres
- Tarpaulin overlap: minimum 1.0 m past each side of breach — NCC 2022 weather-tightness maintained
- Displaced tiles: 4 × tiles recovered and stored on-site
- Damaged tiles: 2 × cracked tiles — bagged and set aside for insurer evidence
- Gutter debris cleared — downflow confirmed before makeshift; bracket repair included in reinstatement

Make-safe scope clearly separated from reinstatement scope per insurer requirement.

## 3. Water Intrusion Extent

Intrusion mapping:

- Roof space: sarking tear 1.8 m² — sarking saturated, requires replacement
- Ceiling insulation (glasswool R3.5): 18 m² saturated in master bedroom/hallway ceiling void — requires replacement
- Plasterboard ceiling master bedroom: 14 m² wet, moisture 78–95% WME — buckled, requires replacement
- Plasterboard ceiling hallway: 8 m² wet, moisture 42–65% WME — assess for drying vs replacement after Day 3
- Master bedroom walls: lower 150 mm moisture elevation (wicking from ceiling) — monitor
- Master bedroom carpet: water intrusion via ceiling — 14 m² removed

## 4. Affected Materials Assessment

| Material | Area | Condition | Action |
|---|---|---|---|
| Glasswool insulation R3.5 | 18 m² | Saturated | Remove |
| Plasterboard ceiling (master bedroom) | 14 m² | Buckled, >70% WME | Remove |
| Plasterboard ceiling (hallway) | 8 m² | Wet, 42–65% WME | Monitor Day 3 |
| Roof sarking (reflective foil) | 1.8 m² | Torn | Replace |
| Carpet + underlay (master bedroom) | 14 m² | Wet | Removed Day 1 |
| Timber battens | Visual inspection | Intact — no rot visible | Dry in place |

## 5. Structural Drying Setup

Affected area with active drying: master bedroom 14 m² + hallway 8 m² = 22 m².

| Equipment | Quantity | Basis |
|---|---|---|
| LGR Dehumidifier | 1 × 70 L/day | 1 per 40 m² |
| Air movers (axial) | 3 × 1.5 kW | 1 per 15 m² + 1 × ceiling void direction |
| HEPA Air Scrubber | Not required | Category 1 storm water, no contamination |

Air mover placement: 2 × directed at exposed ceiling framing (after plasterboard removal); 1 × hallway directed at remaining wet ceiling from below through probe holes.

Drying target per IICRC S500:2025 **§7.3** (Class 2): timber battens ≤16% WME, plasterboard ≤17% WME.

## 6. Contents at Risk

- Master bedroom: bed frame and mattress elevated on foam blocks — no water contact confirmed
- Built-in wardrobe: lower 200 mm moisture detected — contents removed by owner, wardrobe base plates to be monitored
- Electronics: bedside lamps and phone charger — no water contact; functional
- No priority contents (artwork, documents, electronics) in affected zone requiring pack-out

## 7. Reinstatement Scope

Licensed trades required per NCC 2022:

- **Roofing contractor**: replace 2 × cracked terracotta tiles (like-for-like match — Duncan Columbine Monier profile confirmed), re-bed and point 3 × ridge cap sections, replace 1.8 m² sarking (Bradford Anticon or equivalent), repair gutter brackets (2 × stainless screwed brackets)
- **Builder**: replace plasterboard ceiling master bedroom (14 m²) and hallway (8 m² — post-drying confirmation), repair any damaged battens, replace insulation batts R3.5 (18 m²)
- **Painter**: ceiling and cornice painting to affected areas — insurer assessor sign-off required before painting commences
- **Flooring contractor**: carpet replacement master bedroom 14 m² (like-for-like spec per insurer approval)

Insurance assessor pre-reinstatement inspection triggered at issuance of Drying Goal Certificate.
